code: @Manual{,   title = {rustyfilters: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays},   author = {Hugh Graham},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/belian-earth/rustyfilters}, }

what: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays
title: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays
text: Minimal, blazing-fast moving-window filters R matrices, 3-D arrays terra SpatRaster objects, powered Rust rayon. Speckle filters SAR intensity data: rf_lee(), rf_enhanced_lee(), rf_lee_sigma(), rf_frost(), rf_kuan(), rf_gamma_map(). Smoothing: rf_mean() (boxcar), rf_gaussian() (separable), rf_median(). Focal statistics: rf_focal() min, max, range, sd, sum, mode. Parallel default: cores load; tune rf_set_threads(). Results bitwise identical whatever thread count. NA-aware default: missing cells excluded every window statistic (na_policy = "omit"), documented fast path (na_policy = "propagate") compiles NA handling inner loop entirely: NA window makes cell NA. Choose data missing values. Four edge policies: "shrink" (default), "reflect", "nearest", "constant". Mean, sum, sd single-pass speckle filters (Lee, enhanced Lee, Kuan, Gamma-MAP) run separable sliding-sum engine whose per-cell cost independent window size, 21 x 21 window costs 3 x 3.

title: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays
text: Building source needs Rust toolchain (rustc >= 1.80): https://rustup.rs.

title: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays
text: Focal statistics smoothing share engine:

title: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays
text: SpatRaster methods available whenever terra installed (raster materialised memory, filtered layer layer, rebuilt geometry):
code: r <- terra::rast(system.file("ex/elev.tif", package = "terra")) rf_median(r, window = 5L) #> class       : SpatRaster #> size        : 90, 95, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) #> resolution  : 0.008333333, 0.008333333  (x, y) #> extent      : 5.741667, 6.533333, 49.44167, 50.19167  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) #> coord. ref. : lon/lat WGS 84 (EPSG:4326) #> source(s)   : memory #> name        : elevation #> min value   :       142 #> max value   :     537.5

title: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays
text: Everything runs cores default. Control globally: Set options(rustyfilters.threads = n) RUSTYFILTERS_NUM_THREADS loading change startup default.

title: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays
text: Generic convolution (rf_convolve()), Sobel Laplacian edge filters Bilateral guided edge-preserving smoothers Improved Lee sigma (Lee et al. 2009) Histogram/van Herk O(1) median, min max

title: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays
text: build tooling follows extendr / rextendr template.

title: Enhanced Lee speckle filter — rf_enhanced_lee
text: enhanced Lee filter (Lopes et al. 1990) splits window three regimes variation coefficient ci: homogeneous windows (ci <= cu) take window mean, heterogeneous windows blend mean centre exponential damping weight, point targets (ci >= cmax = sqrt(1 + 2/looks)) preserved untouched.

title: Enhanced Lee speckle filter — rf_enhanced_lee
code: rf_enhanced_lee(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'matrix' rf_enhanced_lee(   x,   window = 7L,   looks = 1,   damping = 1,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'array' rf_enhanced_lee(   x,   window = 7L,   looks = 1,   damping = 1,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # Default S3 method rf_enhanced_lee(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'SpatRaster' rf_enhanced_lee(x, ...)

title: Enhanced Lee speckle filter — rf_enhanced_lee
text: x numeric matrix 3-D array (filtered layer layer). Methods terra SpatRaster objects provided terra installed. ... Passed methods. window Window size cells: single odd positive integer, pair c(rows, cols) odd positive integers. looks Single positive number: effective number looks (ENL) intensity image. Controls assumed speckle strength cu = 1 / sqrt(looks); single-look SAR intensity looks = 1. damping Single positive number controlling quickly blend moves towards observed value heterogeneity grows. Larger values preserve detail; 1 conventional default. edge treat windows overhang matrix edge: "shrink" (default) truncates window cells exist; "reflect" mirrors matrix across boundary; "nearest" repeats closest edge cell; "constant" pads edge_value. edge_value Single number used pad edge = "constant". NA allowed behaves missing data na_policy = "omit". na_policy treat missing values inside window. default "omit" excludes statistics (like na.rm = TRUE); window valid cells yields NA. "propagate" fast path: per-cell NA handling compiled inner loop, NA window makes result NA. Use input missing values (spreading NA acceptable) maximum speed.

title: Enhanced Lee speckle filter — rf_enhanced_lee
text: object class dimensions x (dimnames preserved), containing filtered values doubles.

title: Enhanced Lee speckle filter — rf_enhanced_lee
text: Lopes, ., Touzi, R., & Nezry, E. (1990). Adaptive speckle filters scene heterogeneity. IEEE Transactions Geoscience Remote Sensing, 28(6), 992-1000.

what: Focal statistics over a moving window — rf_focal
title: Focal statistics over a moving window — rf_focal
text: Computes summary statistic cells moving window centred cell. statistics share multi-threaded engine; see rf_mean(), rf_median() rf_gaussian() dedicated smoothing filters rf_lee() friends adaptive speckle filters.

title: Focal statistics over a moving window — rf_focal
code: rf_focal(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'matrix' rf_focal(   x,   window = 3L,   stat = c("mean", "median", "min", "max", "range", "sd", "sum", "mode"),   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'array' rf_focal(   x,   window = 3L,   stat = c("mean", "median", "min", "max", "range", "sd", "sum", "mode"),   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # Default S3 method rf_focal(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'SpatRaster' rf_focal(x, ...)

title: Focal statistics over a moving window — rf_focal
text: x numeric matrix 3-D array (filtered layer layer). Methods terra SpatRaster objects provided terra installed. ... Passed methods. window Window size cells: single odd positive integer, pair c(rows, cols) odd positive integers. stat Statistic compute window. One "mean", "median", "min", "max", "range" (max minus min), "sd" (sample standard deviation), "sum" "mode". edge treat windows overhang matrix edge: "shrink" (default) truncates window cells exist; "reflect" mirrors matrix across boundary; "nearest" repeats closest edge cell; "constant" pads edge_value. edge_value Single number used pad edge = "constant". NA allowed behaves missing data na_policy = "omit". na_policy treat missing values inside window. default "omit" excludes statistics (like na.rm = TRUE); window valid cells yields NA. "propagate" fast path: per-cell NA handling compiled inner loop, NA window makes result NA. Use input missing values (spreading NA acceptable) maximum speed.

title: Focal statistics over a moving window — rf_focal
text: object class dimensions x (dimnames preserved), containing filtered values doubles.

title: Focal statistics over a moving window — rf_focal
text: "mode" returns frequent value using exact floating-point equality, ties resolved lowest value. intended categorical data encoded numbers meaningful continuous data.

title: Focal statistics over a moving window — rf_focal
Frost speckle filter — rf_frost
text: Frost (1982) filter convolves window damped exponential kernel exp(-B * d) d distance centre B = damping * ci^2 adapts local variation coefficient: homogeneous windows average broadly, heterogeneous windows concentrate weight centre pixel.

title: Frost speckle filter — rf_frost
code: rf_frost(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'matrix' rf_frost(   x,   window = 7L,   damping = 2,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'array' rf_frost(   x,   window = 7L,   damping = 2,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # Default S3 method rf_frost(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'SpatRaster' rf_frost(x, ...)

title: Frost speckle filter — rf_frost
text: x numeric matrix 3-D array (filtered layer layer). Methods terra SpatRaster objects provided terra installed. ... Passed methods. window Window size cells: single odd positive integer, pair c(rows, cols) odd positive integers. damping Single positive number scaling kernel decay. Larger values preserve edges smooth less; 2 common default. edge treat windows overhang matrix edge: "shrink" (default) truncates window cells exist; "reflect" mirrors matrix across boundary; "nearest" repeats closest edge cell; "constant" pads edge_value. edge_value Single number used pad edge = "constant". NA allowed behaves missing data na_policy = "omit". na_policy treat missing values inside window. default "omit" excludes statistics (like na.rm = TRUE); window valid cells yields NA. "propagate" fast path: per-cell NA handling compiled inner loop, NA window makes result NA. Use input missing values (spreading NA acceptable) maximum speed.

title: Frost speckle filter — rf_frost
text: object class dimensions x (dimnames preserved), containing filtered values doubles.

title: Frost speckle filter — rf_frost
text: Frost, V. S., Stiles, J. ., Shanmugan, K. S., & Holtzman, J. C. (1982). model radar images application adaptive digital filtering multiplicative noise. IEEE Transactions Pattern Analysis Machine Intelligence, 4(2), 157-166.

title: Gamma-MAP speckle filter — rf_gamma_map
text: Gamma maximum posteriori filter (Lopes et al. 1990), assuming Gamma-distributed signal speckle. Homogeneous windows (ci <= cu) take window mean, point targets (ci >= cmax) preserved, MAP estimate solves = (B * m + sqrt(m^2 * B^2 + 4 * alpha * looks * m * x)) / (2 * alpha) alpha = (1 + cu^2) / (ci^2 - cu^2) B = alpha - looks - 1.

title: Gamma-MAP speckle filter — rf_gamma_map
code: rf_gamma_map(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'matrix' rf_gamma_map(   x,   window = 7L,   looks = 1,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'array' rf_gamma_map(   x,   window = 7L,   looks = 1,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # Default S3 method rf_gamma_map(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'SpatRaster' rf_gamma_map(x, ...)

title: Gamma-MAP speckle filter — rf_gamma_map
text: x numeric matrix 3-D array (filtered layer layer). Methods terra SpatRaster objects provided terra installed. ... Passed methods. window Window size cells: single odd positive integer, pair c(rows, cols) odd positive integers. looks Single positive number: effective number looks (ENL) intensity image. Controls assumed speckle strength cu = 1 / sqrt(looks); single-look SAR intensity looks = 1. edge treat windows overhang matrix edge: "shrink" (default) truncates window cells exist; "reflect" mirrors matrix across boundary; "nearest" repeats closest edge cell; "constant" pads edge_value. edge_value Single number used pad edge = "constant". NA allowed behaves missing data na_policy = "omit". na_policy treat missing values inside window. default "omit" excludes statistics (like na.rm = TRUE); window valid cells yields NA. "propagate" fast path: per-cell NA handling compiled inner loop, NA window makes result NA. Use input missing values (spreading NA acceptable) maximum speed.

title: Gamma-MAP speckle filter — rf_gamma_map
text: object class dimensions x (dimnames preserved), containing filtered values doubles.

title: Gamma-MAP speckle filter — rf_gamma_map
text: Lopes, ., Nezry, E., Touzi, R., & Laur, H. (1990). Maximum posteriori speckle filtering first order texture models SAR images. IGARSS 1990, 2409-2412.

title: Gaussian blur — rf_gaussian
text: Smooths separable Gaussian kernel: two 1-D passes, cost grows window edge length rather area. na_policy = "omit" normalized convolution: missing cells excluded remaining weights rescaled, also fills isolated NA holes neighbourhood estimate.

title: Gaussian blur — rf_gaussian
code: rf_gaussian(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'matrix' rf_gaussian(   x,   sigma = 1,   window = NULL,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'array' rf_gaussian(   x,   sigma = 1,   window = NULL,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # Default S3 method rf_gaussian(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'SpatRaster' rf_gaussian(x, ...)

title: Gaussian blur — rf_gaussian
text: x numeric matrix 3-D array (filtered layer layer). Methods terra SpatRaster objects provided terra installed. ... Passed methods. sigma Single positive number: Gaussian standard deviation cells. window Kernel size cells: single odd positive integer pair c(rows, cols). default NULL uses 2 * ceiling(3 * sigma) + 1 dimensions, captures effectively kernel mass. edge treat windows overhang matrix edge: "shrink" (default) truncates window cells exist; "reflect" mirrors matrix across boundary; "nearest" repeats closest edge cell; "constant" pads edge_value. edge_value Single number used pad edge = "constant". NA allowed behaves missing data na_policy = "omit". na_policy treat missing values inside window. default "omit" excludes statistics (like na.rm = TRUE); window valid cells yields NA. "propagate" fast path: per-cell NA handling compiled inner loop, NA window makes result NA. Use input missing values (spreading NA acceptable) maximum speed.

title: Gaussian blur — rf_gaussian
text: object class dimensions x (dimnames preserved), containing filtered values doubles.

title: Kuan speckle filter — rf_kuan
text: Kuan (1985) filter: like rf_lee() derived without linearising multiplicative noise model, giving weight W = (1 - cu^2/ci^2) / (1 + cu^2) (clamped [0, 1]).

title: Kuan speckle filter — rf_kuan
code: rf_kuan(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'matrix' rf_kuan(   x,   window = 7L,   looks = 1,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'array' rf_kuan(   x,   window = 7L,   looks = 1,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # Default S3 method rf_kuan(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'SpatRaster' rf_kuan(x, ...)

title: Kuan speckle filter — rf_kuan
text: x numeric matrix 3-D array (filtered layer layer). Methods terra SpatRaster objects provided terra installed. ... Passed methods. window Window size cells: single odd positive integer, pair c(rows, cols) odd positive integers. looks Single positive number: effective number looks (ENL) intensity image. Controls assumed speckle strength cu = 1 / sqrt(looks); single-look SAR intensity looks = 1. edge treat windows overhang matrix edge: "shrink" (default) truncates window cells exist; "reflect" mirrors matrix across boundary; "nearest" repeats closest edge cell; "constant" pads edge_value. edge_value Single number used pad edge = "constant". NA allowed behaves missing data na_policy = "omit". na_policy treat missing values inside window. default "omit" excludes statistics (like na.rm = TRUE); window valid cells yields NA. "propagate" fast path: per-cell NA handling compiled inner loop, NA window makes result NA. Use input missing values (spreading NA acceptable) maximum speed.

title: Kuan speckle filter — rf_kuan
text: object class dimensions x (dimnames preserved), containing filtered values doubles.

title: Kuan speckle filter — rf_kuan
text: Kuan, D. T., Sawchuk, . ., Strand, T. C., & Chavel, P. (1985). Adaptive noise smoothing filter images signal-dependent noise. IEEE Transactions Pattern Analysis Machine Intelligence, 7(2), 165-177.

title: Lee speckle filter — rf_lee
text: Lee (1980) minimum mean-square-error filter: pixel becomes m + W * (x - m) weight W = v / (v + (m * cu)^2), homogeneous areas take window mean high-contrast features stay close observed value.

title: Lee speckle filter — rf_lee
code: rf_lee(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'matrix' rf_lee(   x,   window = 7L,   looks = 1,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'array' rf_lee(   x,   window = 7L,   looks = 1,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # Default S3 method rf_lee(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'SpatRaster' rf_lee(x, ...)

title: Lee speckle filter — rf_lee
text: x numeric matrix 3-D array (filtered layer layer). Methods terra SpatRaster objects provided terra installed. ... Passed methods. window Window size cells: single odd positive integer, pair c(rows, cols) odd positive integers. looks Single positive number: effective number looks (ENL) intensity image. Controls assumed speckle strength cu = 1 / sqrt(looks); single-look SAR intensity looks = 1. edge treat windows overhang matrix edge: "shrink" (default) truncates window cells exist; "reflect" mirrors matrix across boundary; "nearest" repeats closest edge cell; "constant" pads edge_value. edge_value Single number used pad edge = "constant". NA allowed behaves missing data na_policy = "omit". na_policy treat missing values inside window. default "omit" excludes statistics (like na.rm = TRUE); window valid cells yields NA. "propagate" fast path: per-cell NA handling compiled inner loop, NA window makes result NA. Use input missing values (spreading NA acceptable) maximum speed.

title: Lee speckle filter — rf_lee
text: object class dimensions x (dimnames preserved), containing filtered values doubles.

title: Lee speckle filter — rf_lee
text: Cells whose centre value NA remain NA. Speckle filters expect intensity (linear power) data, dB.

title: Lee speckle filter — rf_lee
text: Lee, J.-S. (1980). Digital image enhancement noise filtering use local statistics. IEEE Transactions Pattern Analysis Machine Intelligence, 2(2), 165-168.

title: Lee sigma speckle filter — rf_lee_sigma
text: classic two-sigma Lee filter (1983): pixel replaced mean window pixels lying within x * (1 +/- k * cu), range pure-speckle observation centre value span. fewer min_count pixels qualify, full window mean used instead (suppressing isolated dark/bright noise).

title: Lee sigma speckle filter — rf_lee_sigma
code: rf_lee_sigma(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'matrix' rf_lee_sigma(   x,   window = 7L,   looks = 1,   k = 2,   min_count = 3L,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'array' rf_lee_sigma(   x,   window = 7L,   looks = 1,   k = 2,   min_count = 3L,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # Default S3 method rf_lee_sigma(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'SpatRaster' rf_lee_sigma(x, ...)

title: Lee sigma speckle filter — rf_lee_sigma
text: x numeric matrix 3-D array (filtered layer layer). Methods terra SpatRaster objects provided terra installed. ... Passed methods. window Window size cells: single odd positive integer, pair c(rows, cols) odd positive integers. looks Single positive number: effective number looks (ENL) intensity image. Controls assumed speckle strength cu = 1 / sqrt(looks); single-look SAR intensity looks = 1. k Single positive number: sigma multiplier defining inclusion range. 2 covers roughly 95.5% pure speckle. min_count Single positive integer: minimum number -range pixels filter falls back full window mean. edge treat windows overhang matrix edge: "shrink" (default) truncates window cells exist; "reflect" mirrors matrix across boundary; "nearest" repeats closest edge cell; "constant" pads edge_value. edge_value Single number used pad edge = "constant". NA allowed behaves missing data na_policy = "omit". na_policy treat missing values inside window. default "omit" excludes statistics (like na.rm = TRUE); window valid cells yields NA. "propagate" fast path: per-cell NA handling compiled inner loop, NA window makes result NA. Use input missing values (spreading NA acceptable) maximum speed.

title: Lee sigma speckle filter — rf_lee_sigma
text: object class dimensions x (dimnames preserved), containing filtered values doubles.

title: Lee sigma speckle filter — rf_lee_sigma
text: Lee, J.-S. (1983). Digital image smoothing sigma filter. Computer Vision, Graphics, Image Processing, 24(2), 255-269.

title: Moving-window mean (boxcar) filter — rf_mean
text: Smooths replacing cell mean window. Runs separable sliding-sum engine, cost per cell independent window size.

title: Moving-window mean (boxcar) filter — rf_mean
code: rf_mean(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'matrix' rf_mean(   x,   window = 3L,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'array' rf_mean(   x,   window = 3L,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # Default S3 method rf_mean(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'SpatRaster' rf_mean(x, ...)

title: Moving-window mean (boxcar) filter — rf_mean
text: x numeric matrix 3-D array (filtered layer layer). Methods terra SpatRaster objects provided terra installed. ... Passed methods. window Window size cells: single odd positive integer, pair c(rows, cols) odd positive integers. edge treat windows overhang matrix edge: "shrink" (default) truncates window cells exist; "reflect" mirrors matrix across boundary; "nearest" repeats closest edge cell; "constant" pads edge_value. edge_value Single number used pad edge = "constant". NA allowed behaves missing data na_policy = "omit". na_policy treat missing values inside window. default "omit" excludes statistics (like na.rm = TRUE); window valid cells yields NA. "propagate" fast path: per-cell NA handling compiled inner loop, NA window makes result NA. Use input missing values (spreading NA acceptable) maximum speed.

title: Moving-window mean (boxcar) filter — rf_mean
text: object class dimensions x (dimnames preserved), containing filtered values doubles.

title: Moving-window median filter — rf_median
text: Replaces cell median window: robust smoother preserves edges removes salt--pepper noise. Windows even number valid cells (possible edges around missing values) average two middle values, matching stats::median().

title: Moving-window median filter — rf_median
code: rf_median(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'matrix' rf_median(   x,   window = 3L,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # S3 method for class 'array' rf_median(   x,   window = 3L,   edge = c("shrink", "reflect", "nearest", "constant"),   edge_value = 0,   na_policy = c("omit", "propagate"),   ... )  # Default S3 method rf_median(x, ...)  # S3 method for class 'SpatRaster' rf_median(x, ...)

what: Control the number of threads used by rustyfilters — rf_set_threads
title: Control the number of threads used by rustyfilters — rf_set_threads
text: filters process windows parallel via rayon. load thread count defaults available cores, unless overridden option(rustyfilters.threads = n) RUSTYFILTERS_NUM_THREADS environment variable (checked order), capped 2 R running checks _R_CHECK_LIMIT_CORES_ set. Set 1 sequential processing zero threading overhead.

title: Control the number of threads used by rustyfilters — rf_set_threads
Control the number of threads used by rustyfilters — rf_set_threads
text: n Integer scalar. Number threads. Must >= 1.

title: Control the number of threads used by rustyfilters — rf_set_threads
text: rf_set_threads() invisibly returns previous thread count; rf_get_threads() returns current thread count integer scalar.

title: Control the number of threads used by rustyfilters — rf_set_threads

what: rustyfilters: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays — rustyfilters-package
title: rustyfilters: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays — rustyfilters-package
text: Minimal, high-performance moving-window filters numeric matrices, 3-D arrays , optionally, terra SpatRaster objects, powered Rust via extendr multi-threading rayon.

title: rustyfilters: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays — rustyfilters-package
text: rf_lee() — Lee (1980) minimum mean-square-error filter rf_enhanced_lee() — enhanced Lee (Lopes et al. 1990) rf_lee_sigma() — Lee sigma (1983) two-sigma filter rf_frost() — Frost (1982) damped exponential filter rf_kuan() — Kuan (1985) filter rf_gamma_map() — Gamma maximum posteriori (Lopes et al. 1990)

title: rustyfilters: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays — rustyfilters-package
text: rf_mean() — moving-window (boxcar) mean rf_gaussian() — separable Gaussian blur rf_median() — moving-window median

title: rustyfilters: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays — rustyfilters-package
text: rf_focal() — min, max, range, standard deviation, sum, mode friends moving window

title: rustyfilters: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays — rustyfilters-package
text: rf_set_threads() / rf_get_threads() — multi-threading control

title: rustyfilters: Fast Focal and Speckle Filters for Matrices and Arrays — rustyfilters-package
text: Maintainer: Hugh Graham hugh@belian.earth Authors: Hugh Graham hugh@belian.earth

title: rustyfilters 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial release: SAR speckle filters (rf_lee(), rf_enhanced_lee(), rf_lee_sigma(), rf_frost(), rf_kuan(), rf_gamma_map()), smoothing filters (rf_mean(), rf_gaussian(), rf_median()) focal statistics (rf_focal()) matrices, 3-D arrays terra SpatRaster objects, thread control via rf_set_threads().
